# Machine learning

Machine learning methods could be categorized under numerical algorithms, but are typically data-driven. Here we bundle some introductionary exercises which use mostly pure Python + Numpy to understand the working of the algorithms. Further, we will also compare with solutions that employ specialized packages such as `scikit-learn`, `Keras`, `pytorch`, `Jax`, etc.

## Accessing datasets

## 1D inference by least squares (Spring 2026)

## CNN: Convolutional Neural Network (Spring 2026)

CNN's are networks which are ideal for signal processing tasks, e.g. object recognition in images, peak detection in 1D signals. This is because the "convolutional" aspect makes the position of the o 

## LSTM: Long Short-Term Memory network (Spring 2026)

A LSTM is a Long Short-Term Memory network.
